issue: Is this repair warranty?
message: I never know if something is covered
until we get guidance from the shop.
We need to know the Primary Failure.
Once they know what failed and why,
they can better determine if it is
going to be filed for warranty.
We will also double check when the
invoice and estimates arrive.

issue: How can I get the fault codes on my Volvo?
message: I would like you to call Volvo Action
line and retrieve the codes.
The number is 1-800-528-6586.
Extensions 1, then 3, then 1 again.
Let me know the codes and the
descriptions they give you please. We
will make an action plan when we know
what we are dealing with.

issue: How do I do a Key Cycle?
message: To do a Key Cycle:
Step 1:
Truck off, Key in the 'ON' position, 5
min, THEN...
Step 2:
Truck off, Key in the 'OFF' position,
5 min.
Let me know if and when you finish.

issue: How do I clear the codes with the battery switch?
message: Turn off the main power to the truck
using the main power switch to the
left of your driver's seat. Turn it
off for 10 seconds, then back on for
10 seconds. Do that 5 times in a row
and let me know if there is any
change. If not, turn off the main
power for 35 min. Any less and we will
not get a full reset, any more and we
will have diminishing returns.

issue: How do I clear the codes by disconnecting the battery?
message: If the power switch did not solve the
issue, we can try one more thing
before we send out service. If you are
able, disconnect the Positive and
Negative battery cables in your
battery box. There are two. They come
in on one side of the box or the
other. Leave them disconnected for 35
min. Please let me know if you are
able and if that gets you rolling.

issue: How do I high Idle the truck?
message: Turn ignition off for about 2 mins.
After restarting:
1. On the dash go to Vehicle Settings
(truck symbol).
2. Scroll down to the bottom and
select Idle Adjust.
3. Press OK.
4. Scroll up or down to adjust the
Idle.
5. set at 900-925 RPM.

issue: I cant get the 5th wheel to unlatch
message: I'd like you to drive around in a
Figure 8 about 3 times. Then try
again. If that does not work.
I'd like you to set the trailer brake
and put the truck in reverse.
Set the truck brakes when the
truck and trailer are as close to one
another as possible. If you have a
dock door, that is the best way to
have a backstop for the trailer.

issue: My Windows will not go up or down
message: In your fuse box there is a fuse that
says Door and window. look for that
decription in the legend on the back
of the fuse box lid. if you remove the
fuse and reinsert it, it will reset
the door module and you should be able
to roll the window up or down.
The fuse box is on the passenger side.
where the glovebox would be if it
were a car   F69 F70 fuse.

issue: My Inverter is not working
message: Start the truck for about 10 - 15 min
to charge up the battery. After it is
charged, if you have the Tundra
inverter (The one big silver box with
the red rocker on-off switch) put your
finger in the middle of the switch.
Turn it on and off as fast as you are
able. seriously, fast. about 10 times.

message: ARKANSAS
CONNECTICUT (up to 25 miles)
GEORGIA
INDIANA(up to 50 miles)
IOWA
KANSAS
MASSACHUSETTS

issue: Alternator not charging
message: Your alternator is no longer charging
the batteries in your reefer unit. It
is an Issue. Essentially, the more it
runs, the faster it will die. If you
have jumper cables, we can attach them
to the truck and it can temporarily
keep the trailer charged up. if not
we need to get you to a shop.

issue: What is a defrost?
message: Your trailer is mechanically sound
and working as expected.  When we have
a load below 32 degrees, We need to
defrost the coils periodically. The
colder the set point and the hotter
the ambient, you will have more
defrosts in route. Defrosts don't
defrost the Trailer box, They defrost
the coils.

issue: My Reefer engine is stalled!
message: Your reefer unit engine is stalled
The first thing we are going to do is
check your fuel level. If you are low
on fuel, add some. If your fuel is
not low, clear the codes. Use the
screen to find the alerts and clear
them. This can happen periodically
and may not be a fault that returns
but if it continues to stall, it may
be a deeper issue that needs a shop.

issue: My Reefer is not getting down to temp
message: Your reefer is operating just as it should.  It is most likely hot product that was loaded into the trailer. When you have produce, eggs, or anything with a high water content, even meat, it will take a lot longer to get into temp. There is nothing we can do but wait. Side note: We do not want to adjust the temps to get it to cool faster.

issue: My trailer temps are all over the place
message: Your reefer is operating as intended. No need for service. You have a product with a lot of packaging. I can see the temp getting in range, the reefer shuts off, and then a 'see saw' pattern where it heats up quickly again. This is a sign of heavy packaging. or a sign that the product is not responding to the supply temp.

issue: My Thermoking Optisets are not showing, empty product list
message: Turn the reefer all the way off for 60 seconds, then turn it back on. Go to products and try to set the reefer correctly. Power cycling it should force it to update to the latest software. If this does not work, please let me know.

issue: My Reefer has a Low Oil Fault
message: ALERT RESPONSE*I am getting alerts about LOW OIL PRESSURE on your reefer. When you are able, please turn off the reefer unit for 5 min and check the oil level. Add only to the SAFE mark, 10 w 30 same as the truck. I would rather it be a little low than overfull.

issue: My Reefer has a Low fuel
message: ALERT RESPONSE*Your reefer is sendings a LOW FUEL LEVEL WARNING. When you are able, please fuel the reefer tank. Please keep in mind, the reefer will shut down when it reaches 3 gallons to prevent it from running out completely. We need you to respond that this message has been received please.

issue: My Reefer says High comp Discharge Temp
message: The reefer is too hot. It is telling us it is overheating. If possible, Id like you to wash out the condenser on the top of the unit. But if you have no access to a water hose, Id like you to Dolly down the legs, Disconnect, pull up 5 ft. Open ONLY the front doors where the reefer motor is. Turn off the unit for 10 min. and let it cool after 10 MIN turn it back on.

issue: My Tandems will not slide
message: 1. Start the truck, set your brakes and build air to the gov. cut off. 2. push in the trailer air supply. Leave the truck on and running for 15 min, fully filling the trailer. 3. Slide as you normally would. Engage the trailer brake, pull the slider pin and try to slide. If all the pins are retracting and the wheels are still rolling when you try, let me know.

issue: My trailer has an air leak
message: I would like you to start the truck and build air until the Governer cuts out. Leave your brakes set on truck and trailer. Once you build air all the way, shut off the truck, supply air to the trailer. Look listen and feel for leaks. If you find a leak, send me a picture.

issue: My trailer tire inflation light is on
message: Check the tire inflation lines. They go from the Valve stem to the center of the axle. Just hand tighten, to the right. both ends. That may stop the leak. Check for nails in the tires. Listen for a leak under the trailer. Push in the red knob. If you hear air leaking, after the bags have inflated, let me know where you hear it leaking from.

issue: What set of drive tires do we chain?
message: The rear set of tires on the drive
axle are the ones that you chain. They
are the ones that are connected to
the drivetrain and rotate when you
accelerate. The front drives do not
turn when the fuel pedal is depressed
BUT will if you apply the inner axle
lock. We do not want to use the inner
axle lock if possible.

issue: What are the Chains and Cables for?
message: You chain the truck and you put
cables on the trailer. The chains are
designed to help you keep forward
momentum and the cables are designed
to keep the trailer from moving side
to side. In most cases, you just need
to chain one set of each, however
local and state laws may require all
drives to be chained. Tire socks are
also good for the drive tires

issue: How much Antigel do I use?
message: Follow the directions on the back of
the bottle. It will let you know how
much to add to each tank. We use many
brands and the dosage is different for
each. It is best to add when you are
fueling to be sure it mixes well. Run
your apu and heater after to get some
in those lines as they are more
susceptible to gel up. Dont assume fuel
stops treat the fuel.

issue: Where can I get Chains and Cables?
message: Most truck stops will carry chains
cables and tire socks. Even though you
may not use them, some states require
you carry them by law. You will get a
hefty fine if you roll thru certain
areas in the winter and they find you
have none on you. you can also get
them at the best price at a terminal.

issue: I'm stuck, how do I use the inneraxle lock?
message: Make sure you are at a complete stop.
The wheels should be totally still
before you engage the inner axel lock.
When you press the fuel, all the tires
will turn together. This should help
get you unstuck. When you have cleared
the place where you have no traction
come to a complete stop and disable
the lock. Do not roll any further than
you must with it engaged.

issue: I'm gelled up!
message: If your fuel is gelled in the truck
or reefer tank, you can try to use
Diesel 911. Red Bottle to get it
ungelled. If it has reached the fuel
filter, that will need to be changed.
Pick up an extra when you go by a
terminal or shop. You can find the
filter number on the one you currently
have on the truck. If diesel 911 does
not help, we will need road service

issue: What is a Drive Cycle?
message: A drive cycle is: Key turned to the On Position, Driven at Highway speed 40 MPH +, for at least 10 miles, Key turned to the OFF position.

issue: Where can I find the CARB number?
message: https://arber.arb.ca.gov/publicTruSearch.arb

issue: How do I Switch Tabs?
message: CTRL + TAB

issue: How do I Open a new tab?
message: CTRL + T

issue: How do I Close a tab?
message: CTRL + W

issue: How do I Open a new Window?
message: CTRL + N

issue: How do I Close a Window?
message: CTRL + F4

issue: How do I Snap to the Search Bar?
message: CTRL + L
